# NO AI ACT

> NO AI ACT is a free, open-source investigative serious game about the European
> AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689). The player acts as an inspector: they open
> the file of an artificial intelligence system, examine the evidence, cite the
> proof and file an inspection report classifying the system's risk (prohibited,
> high-risk, to be governed, or compliant). It is an educational simulation, not
> legal advice.

Languages: Italian (default) and English.
Audience: upper-secondary schools, universities, professional training, and
professionals working with AI governance.
Pricing: free and open source (MIT code, CC BY 4.0 educational content).
Privacy: no backend, no account, no personal data collection, no remote
analytics by default. Progress is stored only in the browser's localStorage.

## Pages

- [Italian landing page](https://matteoangeloni-jpeg.github.io/-NO-AI-ACT/): overview, what you learn, the 11 cases, audience, FAQ (Italian).
- [English landing page](https://matteoangeloni-jpeg.github.io/-NO-AI-ACT/en/): overview, what you learn, the 11 cases, audience, FAQ (English).
- [Play the game](https://matteoangeloni-jpeg.github.io/-NO-AI-ACT/play/): the interactive game (runs in the browser, no account).

## What it teaches

- Risk classification of AI systems under the European AI Act.
- Prohibited AI practices (e.g. social scoring) and why they are banned.
- High-risk systems (e.g. healthcare, education, public administration) and their obligations.
- Transparency duties, effective human oversight and deployer responsibility.
- Why a disclaimer or a merely formal human check is often not enough.
- How the context of use changes the risk of the same system.
- Contestability: arguing a decision with evidence.

## The 11 cases

1. The city of scores — social scoring and the boundary of prohibited practices.
2. The interview that does not exist — automated recruitment, risks in employment.
3. The synthetic city — generated content and transparency obligations.
4. The observed class — monitoring in education, rights of minors.
5. Invisible triage — high-risk clinical decision support in healthcare.
6. Faces in the crowd — biometric recognition in public spaces.
7. Civic credit — mirror case: prohibited social scoring vs welfare and credit scoring.
8. The desk that always answers — public chatbot, reliance, human channel.
9. The opaque tender — public procurement of AI without documentation or governance.
10. The profiled classroom — adaptive learning platform profiling students.
11. The do-it-all model — generative model (GPAI) used downstream in internal decisions.

## Documentation

- [Project brief](https://github.com/matteoangeloni-jpeg/-NO-AI-ACT/blob/main/docs/NO_AI_ACT_PROJECT_BRIEF.md): goals, scope and design of the game.
- [Teacher quick start](https://github.com/matteoangeloni-jpeg/-NO-AI-ACT/blob/main/docs/TEACHER_QUICK_START.md): how to run it in class.
- [Legal disclaimer](https://github.com/matteoangeloni-jpeg/-NO-AI-ACT/blob/main/docs/LEGAL_DISCLAIMER.md): scope and limits of the simulation.

## Notes

- NO AI ACT is a didactic simplification and does not replace the AI Act text or professional legal advice.
- A third-party legal review is recommended before any formal institutional use.
- Cases such as GPAI, public chatbots, EdTech and procurement are framed by context of use, not as blanket prohibitions.
